zh.lifeSummary: William Hilton I Of Shipbrook by Witton Chapelry, Northwich於從1505年到1510年出生在英國英格蘭爾諾維其。他於從1533年到1539年與Elisabeth在英國英格蘭爾結婚。他們至少是3個兒子的父母。他於1578年住在英國爾。他去世於從1578年到1579年在英國爾希布盧可。

nameMeanings.name: Hilton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: habitational name from any of various places called Hilton (e.g. in Cambridgeshire, Derbyshire, Staffordshire, North Yorkshire, and Westmorland), most of which derive from Old English hyll ‘hill’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’, though some come from Old English helde ‘slope’ + tūn.

occasionally, as in the case of Ralph filius Hilton (Yorkshire 1219), perhaps from the personal name Hilton, itself a possible form of Norman Hildun, composed of the ancient Germanic elements hild ‘strife, battle’ + hūn ‘bear cub’. Compare Hilt . This English surname is present in Ireland (mainly taken to Ulster in the early 17th century, though recorded earlier in Dublin).

variants.name: Hulton
meanings: English (Lancashire): habitational name from Hulton (Lancashire) or Hulton (Staffordshire), both from Old English hyll ‘hill’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. Compare Hilton .

variants.name: Hilt
meanings: German: from a short form of Hildebrand or some other compound ancient Germanic name formed with hild ‘strife, battle’ as the first element. See Hild .
English (London): from the Middle English personal name Hilte, ultimately from ancient Germanic Helto, probably from halith- ‘hero’.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

en.lifeSummary: William Hilton I Of Shipbrook by Witton Chapelry, Northwich was born from 1505 to 1510, in Northwich, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom. He married Elisabeth from 1533 to 1539, in Cheshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons. He lived in Cheshire, England in 1578. He died from 1578 to 1579, in Shipbrook, Cheshire, England.

ko.lifeSummary: William Hilton I Of Shipbrook by Witton Chapelry, Northwich(은)는 1505년 부터 1510년 까지 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 노르스위치에서 태어났다. 그는 1533년 부터 1539년 까지 영국 잉글랜드 체실르에서 Elisabeth(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 1578년도에 영국 체실르에서 살았다. 그는 1578년 부터 1579년 까지 영국 체실르 시브룩에서 사망했다.
